title: Doppler auth tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Doppler Service Token detected (dp.st. prefix). Doppler service tokens provide read-only access to secrets in a specific Doppler project and environment configuration. Exposing this token allows attackers to retrieve all secrets stored in your Doppler environment including API keys, database passwords, and other credentials managed by Doppler. Revoke immediately at dashboard.doppler.com and rotate all exposed downstream secrets.

Doppler CLI Token detected (dp.ct. prefix). Doppler CLI tokens grant access to Doppler's command-line interface to manage projects, environments, and secrets. Exposing this token allows attackers to read, create, and modify secrets across all accessible Doppler projects. Revoke immediately at dashboard.doppler.com/workplace/tokens.

Doppler SCIM Token detected (dp.scim. prefix). Doppler SCIM tokens enable System for Cross-domain Identity Management, allowing automated user provisioning and deprovisioning. Exposing this token allows attackers to manipulate user access controls, add or remove team members, and potentially gain access to your entire Doppler organization.

Doppler Personal Token detected (dp.pt. prefix). Doppler personal tokens grant full access to Doppler's API on behalf of a specific user, including reading and writing secrets across all projects the user can access. Exposing this token allows complete impersonation of the user's Doppler account. Revoke immediately at dashboard.doppler.com/workplace/tokens.
